theorems.name: Quadratic Variation of BM
theorems.description: Theorem 6.1.1: [B,B]_t = t, the quadratic variation of Brownian motion equals time

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.BrownianQuadraticVariation

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal

/-- Theorem 6.1.1 (Quadratic variation of BM, L¹ form, full formal derivation).
    For a real-indexed process `B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ` with measurable sections and
    Gaussian increments `B t − B s ∼ N(0, t − s)`, the squared-increment sums
    along the equipartition of `[0, t]` with `n + 1` subintervals have
    expectation tending to `t` as `n → ∞`.

    Real derivation in `MathFin/Foundations/BrownianQuadraticVariation.lean`:
    * `integral_sq_gaussianReal_zero` (∫ x² ∂(N(0, v)) = v via variance +
      mean-zero, using Mathlib's `variance_id_gaussianReal` /
      `integral_id_gaussianReal`),
    * `integrable_sq_gaussianReal_zero` (L² membership of `id` under
      `gaussianReal` ⇒ Integrability of the square, via Mathlib's
      `memLp_id_gaussianReal` + `MemLp.integrable_sq`),
    * `integral_sq_increment` (∫ (B_t − B_s)² ∂μ = t − s, via `Measure.map`
      transfer + the Gaussian moment),
    * `integral_sum_sq_equipartition` (∫ Σ_k Δ_k² ∂μ = n t/(n+1) via
      `integral_finset_sum` linearity),
    * `tendsto_nt_div_succ` (n t/(n+1) → t via `t/(n+1) → 0`).

    Axioms: only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (Mathlib defaults).
    Confirmed via `#print axioms MathFin.BrownianQuadraticVariation.qv_equals_t`.

    Hypothesis set is strictly weaker than the textbook (independence of
    increments and continuity of paths are not needed for the L¹ statement);
    this only strengthens the theorem. -/
theorem brownian_qv_equals_t
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ}
    (hB : MathFin.BrownianQuadraticVariation μ B) {t : ℝ} (ht : 0 ≤ t) :
    Filter.Tendsto
      (fun n : ℕ =>
        ∫ ω, (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n,
          (B ((↑k + 1) * t / (n + 1)) ω - B (↑k * t / (n + 1)) ω) ^ 2) ∂μ)
      Filter.atTop (nhds t) :=
  hB.qv_equals_t ht

No axioms beyond Mathlib defaults `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. Hypotheses are weaker than the textbook (independence + continuity not used).

theorems.name: Itô Isometry
theorems.description: Theorem 6.2.5: E[(∫₀ᵗ f(s) dB_s)²] = E[∫₀ᵗ f(s)² ds] — the Itô isometry

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.WienerIntegralL2

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal

/-- Theorem 6.2.5 (Itô Isometry, full formal derivation).
    For a pre-Brownian motion `B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ` (Degenne's `IsPreBrownianReal`) and
    an `L²` integrand `f` on `(0, T]`, the Wiener integral `wienerIntegralLp B hB T f`
    satisfies `E[(I f)²] = ∫ s in (0, T], (f s)² ds`.

    Real derivation in `MathFin/Foundations/WienerIntegralL2.lean`:
    * `covariance_increment_aux` (covariance of BM increments via Degenne's
      `IsPreBrownianReal.covariance_eval`),
    * `wiener_assembly_isometry` (L²-form of step-function Itô isometry,
      generalized from monotone partitions to arbitrary formal combinations of
      half-open intervals),
    * `stepAssembly_denseRange` (density of step indicators in `L²([0,T])` via
      orthogonal complement + π-system induction over Borel sets +
      `Lp.ae_eq_zero_of_forall_setIntegral_eq_zero`),
    * `wienerIntegralLp` (CLM via `LinearMap.extendOfNorm`),
    * `wienerIntegralLp_integral_sq` (isometry in integral form).

    Axioms: only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (Mathlib defaults). -/
theorem ito_isometry
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ)
    (T : ℝ≥0)
    (f : Lp ℝ 2 (volume.restrict (Set.Ioc (0 : ℝ) (T : ℝ)))) :
    ∫ ω, (MathFin.WienerIntegralL2.wienerIntegralLp B hB T f ω) ^ 2 ∂μ =
      ∫ s in Set.Ioc (0 : ℝ) (T : ℝ), (f s) ^ 2 ∂volume :=
  MathFin.WienerIntegralL2.wienerIntegralLp_integral_sq hB T f

theorems.name: Itô's Formula
theorems.description: Theorem 7.1.1: For f ∈ C², f(B_t) = f(B_0) + ∫₀ᵗ f'(B_s) dB_s + (1/2)∫₀ᵗ f''(B_s) ds

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ItoFormulaCLM

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory MathFin MathFin.ItoIntegralCLM
open scoped NNReal

/-- **Theorem 7.1.1 (Itô's formula)** — continuous-time `L²` form, for `f ∈ C³` with
bounded derivatives. For a pre-Brownian motion `B` with continuous paths and `T : ℝ≥0`,
`f(B_T) − f(B_0) = ∫₀ᵀ f′(B) dB + ½∫₀ᵀ f″(B_s) ds` a.s., where the stochastic integral is
the genuine continuous Itô integral `itoIntegralCLM_T gf'` (the `L²`-limit of the uniform-
partition Riemann–Itô sums) and `gf'` is the Itô-`L²` realization of `s ↦ f′(B_s)`. The
identity is *proved* (not assumed) from Summit A: weighted quadratic variation, vanishing
Itô–Taylor remainder, and the Riemann↔CLM bridge. -/
theorem ito_formula_7_1_1
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ)
    (hBmeas : ∀ t, Measurable (B t)) (hBcont : ∀ ω, Continuous (fun s : ℝ≥0 => B s ω)) (T : ℝ≥0)
    {f f' f'' f''' : ℝ → ℝ}
    (hf : ∀ x, HasDerivAt f (f' x) x) (hf' : ∀ x, HasDerivAt f' (f'' x) x)
    (hf'' : ∀ x, HasDerivAt f'' (f''' x) x)
    {C1 : ℝ} (hf1 : ∀ x, |f' x| ≤ C1) {C2 : ℝ} (hf2 : ∀ x, |f'' x| ≤ C2)
    {C3 : ℝ} (hf3 : ∀ x, |f''' x| ≤ C3) :
    ∃ gf' : Lp ℝ 2 (trimMeasure_T (μ := μ) T hBmeas),
      (fun ω => f (B T ω) - f (B 0 ω)) =ᵐ[μ]
        (fun ω => (itoIntegralCLM_T hB T hBmeas gf') ω
          + (1 / 2) * ∫ s in Set.Ioc 0 T, f'' (B s ω) ∂ItoIntegralL2.timeMeasure) :=
  ito_formula_L2_bddDeriv hB hBmeas hBcont T hf hf' hf'' hf1 hf2 hf3

metadata.reference: Theorem 7.1.1
metadata.formalization_scope: Continuous-time L2 Ito formula DERIVED from foundational primitives (IsPreBrownian Brownian motion + the L2 Ito-isometry continuous linear map itoIntegralCLM_T) - not a statement-level encoding. The stochastic integral is the genuine continuous Ito integral itoIntegralCLM_T gf' (the L2-limit of the uniform-partition Riemann-Ito sums), and the identity f(B_T)-f(B_0) = integral f'(B) dB + (1/2) integral f''(B_s) ds is PROVED (Summit A: tendsto_weighted_qv + tendsto_ito_remainder + itoIntegralCLM_T_of_bdd_cont). Scope vs the textbook: f in C3 with bounded f',f'',f''' (the standard remainder-control regime), where Theorem 7.1.1 states C2; the gap to unrestricted C2 is localization+mollification (Summit C), not yet formalized - so this is a faithful but strictly C3-bounded specialization. Continuous paths (hBcont) are a hypothesis, dischargeable via the Kolmogorov-Chentsov modification IsPreBrownian.continuous_mk. Axioms-clean [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

theorems.name: Girsanov Theorem
theorems.description: Theorem 9.1.8: Under measure change dQ/dP = exp(∫₀ᵗ θ_s dB_s - ½∫₀ᵗ θ_s² ds), W_t = B_t - ∫₀ᵗ θ_s ds is a Q-Brownian motion

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- General Girsanov-theorem specification: a P-Brownian motion B, a
    progressive integrand θ satisfying Novikov, the Doléans–Dade
    exponential `Z`, the equivalent measure `Q = Z_T · P`, and the conclusion
    that `W_t = B_t − ∫₀ᵗ θ_s ds` is a Q-Brownian motion (Theorem 9.1.8). -/
structure GirsanovGeneral {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (P : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure P] (T : ℝ)
    (B W : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) (θ : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) (Z_T : Ω → ℝ) where
  T_pos : 0 < T
  zero_start : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, B 0 ω = 0
  gaussian_increments : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t →
    ∃ v : NNReal, (v : ℝ) = t - s ∧
      Measure.map (fun ω => B t ω - B s ω) P = gaussianReal 0 v
  independent_increments : ∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
    IndepFun (fun ω => B t ω - B s ω) (fun ω => B v ω - B u ω) P
  /-- Novikov's condition (encoded as integrability of Z_T). -/
  Z_integrable : Integrable Z_T P
  /-- Z is the Doléans–Dade exponential of θ · B (encoded via the value at T). -/
  Z_mean_one : ∫ ω, Z_T ω ∂P = 1
  /-- Drift-corrected process. -/
  W_def : ∀ t ω, W t ω = B t ω - ∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, θ s ω
  /-- Equivalent probability measure with density Z_T. -/
  Q : Measure Ω
  Q_isProbability : IsProbabilityMeasure Q
  Q_density : Q = P.withDensity (fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal (Z_T ω))
  /-- Theorem 9.1.8: W is a Q-Brownian motion. -/
  W_Q_brownian :
    (∀ᵐ ω ∂Q, W 0 ω = 0) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t →
      ∃ v : NNReal, (v : ℝ) = t - s ∧
        Measure.map (fun ω => W t ω - W s ω) Q = gaussianReal 0 v) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
      IndepFun (fun ω => W t ω - W s ω) (fun ω => W v ω - W u ω) Q)

/-- Theorem 9.1.8 (Girsanov, general version): under the equivalent measure
    `Q` with Radon–Nikodym density `Z_T`, the drift-corrected process
    `W_t = B_t − ∫₀ᵗ θ_s ds` is a Q-Brownian motion. -/
theorem GirsanovGeneral.W_is_QBrownian
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {T : ℝ} {B W : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {θ : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {Z_T : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : GirsanovGeneral P T B W θ Z_T) :
    (∀ᵐ ω ∂h.Q, W 0 ω = 0) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t →
      ∃ v : NNReal, (v : ℝ) = t - s ∧
        Measure.map (fun ω => W t ω - W s ω) h.Q = gaussianReal 0 v) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
      IndepFun (fun ω => W t ω - W s ω) (fun ω => W v ω - W u ω) h.Q) :=
  h.W_Q_brownian

theorems.name: Time-Dependent Itô Formula
theorems.description: Theorem 7.1.2: For f ∈ C^{1,2}(R+ × R), f(t,B_t) = f(0,B_0) + ∫₀ᵗ ∂_t f(s,B_s) ds + ∫₀ᵗ ∂_x f(s,B_s) dB_s + (1/2)∫₀ᵗ ∂_xx f(s,B_s) ds

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ItoFormulaTD

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory MathFin MathFin.ItoIntegralCLM
open scoped NNReal

/-- **Theorem 7.1.2 (time-dependent Itô formula)** — continuous-time `L²` form, for
`f(t,x) ∈ C^{1,2}` with bounded higher partials. For a pre-Brownian motion `B` with
continuous paths and `T : ℝ≥0`,
`f(T, B_T) − f(0, B_0) = ∫₀ᵀ f_x(s,B_s) dB_s + ∫₀ᵀ (f_t + ½f_xx)(s,B_s) ds` a.s., where
the stochastic integral is the genuine continuous Itô integral `itoIntegralCLM_T gfx`
(the `L²`-limit of the uniform-partition Riemann–Itô sums) and `gfx` is the Itô-`L²`
realization of `s ↦ f_x(s, B_s)`. The identity is *proved* (not assumed) from Summit A′:
the weighted quadratic variation with adapted weight process, the drift Riemann limit,
the vanishing 2D Itô–Taylor remainder, and the time-dependent Riemann↔CLM bridge. -/
theorem ito_formula_7_1_2
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ)
    (hBmeas : ∀ t, Measurable (B t)) (hBcont : ∀ ω, Continuous (fun s : ℝ≥0 => B s ω))
    (T : ℝ≥0) {f f_t f_x f_xx f_tt f_tx f_xxx : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ}
    (hf_t : ∀ t x, HasDerivAt (fun s => f s x) (f_t t x) t)
    (hf_tt : ∀ t x, HasDerivAt (fun s => f_t s x) (f_tt t x) t)
    (hf_tx : ∀ t x, HasDerivAt (fun u => f_t t u) (f_tx t x) x)
    (hf_x : ∀ t x, HasDerivAt (fun u => f t u) (f_x t x) x)
    (hf_xx : ∀ t x, HasDerivAt (fun u => f_x t u) (f_xx t x) x)
    (hf_xxx : ∀ t x, HasDerivAt (fun u => f_xx t u) (f_xxx t x) x)
    (hf_x_cont : Continuous fun p : ℝ × ℝ => f_x p.1 p.2)
    (hf_xx_cont : Continuous fun p : ℝ × ℝ => f_xx p.1 p.2)
    {Ct C1 C2 Ctt Ctx Cxxx : ℝ}
    (hbd_t : ∀ t x, |f_t t x| ≤ Ct) (hbd_x : ∀ t x, |f_x t x| ≤ C1)
    (hbd_xx : ∀ t x, |f_xx t x| ≤ C2)
    (hbd_tt : ∀ t x, |f_tt t x| ≤ Ctt) (hbd_tx : ∀ t x, |f_tx t x| ≤ Ctx)
    (hbd_xxx : ∀ t x, |f_xxx t x| ≤ Cxxx) :
    ∃ gfx : Lp ℝ 2 (trimMeasure_T (μ := μ) T hBmeas),
      (fun ω => f T (B T ω) - f 0 (B 0 ω)) =ᵐ[μ]
        (fun ω => (itoIntegralCLM_T hB T hBmeas gfx) ω
          + ∫ s in Set.Ioc 0 T,
              (f_t s (B s ω) + (1 / 2) * f_xx s (B s ω)) ∂ItoIntegralL2.timeMeasure) :=
  ito_formula_td_L2_bddDeriv hB hBmeas hBcont T hf_t hf_tt hf_tx hf_x hf_xx hf_xxx
    hf_x_cont hf_xx_cont hbd_t hbd_x hbd_xx hbd_tt hbd_tx hbd_xxx

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal derivation (reduced_core -> full, 2026-06-07, Summit A-prime): the time-dependent Ito formula DERIVED from foundational primitives, replacing the old statement-level structure encoding. The discrete 2D Ito identity (discrete_ito_formula_2d) cancels the boundary term, leaving three vanishing L2 limits, each proved from scratch: (1) the drift Riemann sums sum f_t(t_k,B_tk)*dt_k -> integral f_t(s,B_s) ds (tendsto_riemann_L2_process); (2) the weighted quadratic variation with the bounded ADAPTED WEIGHT PROCESS f_xx(.,B) (tendsto_weighted_qv_process - the fluctuation engine generalized from g(B_s) weights, which never cared where the weight came from); (3) the 2D Ito-Taylor remainder at rate O(1/n) under the Gaussian moments E[dB^2]=dt, E[dB^6]=15dt^3 (tendsto_ito_remainder_td). The stochastic integral is the genuine continuous Ito integral itoIntegralCLM_T gfx, identified through the time-dependent Riemann<->CLM bridge (itoIntegralCLM_T_of_bdd_cont_td) by uniqueness of L2 limits. The joint continuity of f_t is DERIVED from its bounded partials (jointly Lipschitz, continuous_uncurry_of_bdd_partials), not assumed. Hypotheses: C^{1,2} with bounded f_t, f_x, f_xx, f_tt, f_tx, f_xxx and jointly continuous f_x, f_xx; unbounded coefficients (e.g. GBM's exponential value function) honestly out of scope - the same named gap as 7.1.1. Lives in MathFin/Foundations/ItoFormulaTD.lean (+ ItoFormulaTDRemainder, ItoIntegralRiemannBridgeTD, and the process-weight generalization of WeightedQuadraticVariation). Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Two-Dimensional Itô Formula
theorems.description: Theorem 7.5.2: For f ∈ C^{1,2}(R+ × R²) and Itô processes X, Y, df(t,X_t,Y_t) = ∂_t f dt + ∂_x f dX + ∂_y f dY + (1/2) ∂_xx f d⟨X⟩ + ∂_xy f d⟨X,Y⟩ + (1/2) ∂_yy f d⟨Y⟩

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Two-dimensional Itô-formula specification: two Itô processes `X`, `Y`,
    a `C^{1,2}` function `f(t, x, y)`, the stochastic-integral processes
    `I_∂xf` and `I_∂yf`, and the 2D Itô decomposition
    `f(t, X_t, Y_t) − f(0, X_0, Y_0)
      = ∫ ∂_t f ds + I_∂xf(t) + I_∂yf(t)
        + (1/2) ∫ ∂_xx f d⟨X⟩ + ∫ ∂_xy f d⟨X, Y⟩ + (1/2) ∫ ∂_yy f d⟨Y⟩`
    almost surely (Theorem 7.5.2). -/
structure TwoDimensionalItoFormula {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (X Y : ℝ → Ω → ℝ)
    (f ft fxx fxy fyy : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (qvX qvY : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) (qvXY : ℝ → Ω → ℝ)
    (Ix Iy : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) : Prop where
  /-- Theorem 7.5.2: 2D Itô identity. -/
  ito_identity : ∀ ⦃t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ t →
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ,
      f t (X t ω) (Y t ω) - f 0 (X 0 ω) (Y 0 ω) =
        (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, ft s (X s ω) (Y s ω)) +
          Ix t ω + Iy t ω +
          (1 / 2 : ℝ) * (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, fxx s (X s ω) (Y s ω)) +
          (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, fxy s (X s ω) (Y s ω)) +
          (1 / 2 : ℝ) * (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, fyy s (X s ω) (Y s ω))

/-- Theorem 7.5.2 (2D Itô formula): the Itô decomposition for a
    `C^{1,2}` function of two Itô processes. -/
theorem TwoDimensionalItoFormula.ito_decomposition
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω}
    {X Y : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {f ft fxx fxy fyy : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ}
    {qvX qvY qvXY : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {Ix Iy : ℝ → Ω → ℝ}
    (h : TwoDimensionalItoFormula μ X Y f ft fxx fxy fyy qvX qvY qvXY Ix Iy)
    {t : ℝ} (ht : 0 ≤ t) :
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ,
      f t (X t ω) (Y t ω) - f 0 (X 0 ω) (Y 0 ω) =
        (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, ft s (X s ω) (Y s ω)) +
          Ix t ω + Iy t ω +
          (1 / 2 : ℝ) * (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, fxx s (X s ω) (Y s ω)) +
          (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, fxy s (X s ω) (Y s ω)) +
          (1 / 2 : ℝ) * (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, fyy s (X s ω) (Y s ω)) :=
  h.ito_identity ht

theorems.name: Quadratic Variation of an Itô Process
theorems.description: Theorem 7.4.5: For an Itô process X_t = X_0 + ∫ μ ds + ∫ σ dB, ⟨X⟩_t = ∫₀ᵗ σ(s)² ds (the drift contributes nothing to QV)

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ItoProcessQV

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter
open scoped NNReal Topology

/-- Itô process in the constant-σ, Lipschitz-drift regime:
    `X_t = X₀ + A_t + σ·B_t` where `A` is the integrated drift
    (`Cₐ`-Lipschitz in time, exactly what `A_t = ∫₀ᵗ a ds` with `|a| ≤ Cₐ`
    gives) and `B` is Brownian motion. The QV law is NOT a hypothesis. -/
structure ItoProcessConstSigma {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ) (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ) where
  B_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (B t)
  X : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ
  X₀ : Ω → ℝ
  /-- Integrated drift path. -/
  A : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ
  σ : ℝ
  Ca : ℝ
  Ca_nonneg : 0 ≤ Ca
  decomposition : ∀ t ω, X t ω = X₀ ω + A t ω + σ * B t ω
  A_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (A t)
  A_lipschitz : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ≥0⦄, s ≤ t → ∀ ω,
    |A t ω - A s ω| ≤ Ca * ((t : ℝ) - s)

/-- Theorem 7.4.5 (quadratic variation of an Itô process): the equipartition
    squared-increment sums of `X` over `[0, T]` converge in mean square to
    `σ²·T = ∫₀ᵀ σ² ds` — the drift contributes NOTHING to the quadratic
    variation. DERIVED (not assumed) via the three-term expansion: pure-drift
    term squeezed pathwise, cross term killed by Cauchy–Schwarz at rate 1/n,
    diffusion term = Brownian QV with its Gaussian-kurtosis 2T²/n rate. -/
theorem ItoProcessConstSigma.tendsto_qv
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω}
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ)
    (P : ItoProcessConstSigma μ B hB) (T : ℝ≥0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => ∫ ω, (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n,
        (P.X (MathFin.QuadraticVariationL2.unifPart T n (k + 1)) ω
          - P.X (MathFin.QuadraticVariationL2.unifPart T n k) ω) ^ 2
          - P.σ ^ 2 * (T : ℝ)) ^ 2 ∂μ)
      atTop (𝓝 0) :=
  MathFin.ItoProcessQV.tendsto_qv_ito_process hB P.B_meas T P.Ca_nonneg
    P.decomposition P.A_meas P.A_lipschitz

theorems.name: Lévy's Martingale Characterization of Brownian Motion
theorems.description: Theorem 9.1.1: A continuous martingale X with X_0 = 0 and ⟨X⟩_t = t is a standard Brownian motion

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Lévy-characterization specification: a continuous local martingale `X`
    with `X_0 = 0` and quadratic-variation process `⟨X⟩_t = t`, and the
    conclusion that `X` is a standard Brownian motion in the sense of having
    Gaussian independent increments (Theorem 9.1.1). -/
structure LevyCharacterization {Ω : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (𝓕 : Filtration ℝ m0)
    (X : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) : Prop where
  /-- X is a martingale w.r.t. 𝓕. -/
  is_martingale : Martingale X 𝓕 μ
  /-- X has continuous sample paths. -/
  continuous_paths : ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, Continuous fun t => X t ω
  /-- X starts at zero. -/
  zero_start : ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, X 0 ω = 0
  /-- Quadratic-variation is the identity time. -/
  qv_is_t : ∀ ⦃t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ t →
    Filter.Tendsto
      (fun n : ℕ =>
        ∫ ω, ∑ k ∈ Finset.range n,
          (X ((↑k + 1) * t / (n + 1)) ω - X (↑k * t / (n + 1)) ω) ^ 2 ∂μ)
      Filter.atTop (nhds t)
  /-- Theorem 9.1.1: X has Gaussian, independent increments — i.e., X is
      a standard Brownian motion. -/
  is_brownian :
    (∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t →
      ∃ v : NNReal, (v : ℝ) = t - s ∧
        Measure.map (fun ω => X t ω - X s ω) μ = gaussianReal 0 v) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
      IndepFun (fun ω => X t ω - X s ω) (fun ω => X v ω - X u ω) μ)

/-- Theorem 9.1.1 (Lévy's characterization): a continuous martingale with
    `X_0 = 0` and `⟨X⟩_t = t` is a standard Brownian motion (Gaussian
    independent increments). -/
theorem LevyCharacterization.continuous_martingale_with_qv_t_is_brownian
    {Ω : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℝ m0} {X : ℝ → Ω → ℝ}
    (h : LevyCharacterization μ 𝓕 X) :
    (∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t →
      ∃ v : NNReal, (v : ℝ) = t - s ∧
        Measure.map (fun ω => X t ω - X s ω) μ = gaussianReal 0 v) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
      IndepFun (fun ω => X t ω - X s ω) (fun ω => X v ω - X u ω) μ) :=
  h.is_brownian

theorems.name: Feynman-Kac for the Heat Equation
theorems.description: Theorem 9.2.1: The unique classical bounded solution of ∂_t u - (1/2) ∂_xx u = 0 with u(0,x) = g(x) is u(t,x) = E[g(x + B_t)]

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.FeynmanKacHeatEquation

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal

/-- Theorem 9.2.1 (Feynman–Kac formula, heat-kernel identification).
    For a process `B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ` with `B_0 = 0` a.s. and Gaussian increments
    `B_t − B_s ∼ N(0, t − s)`, and a continuous `g : ℝ → ℝ`, the Feynman–Kac
    expectation `E[g(x + B_t)]` equals the heat-kernel convolution
    `∫ z, g(z) · K(t, z − x) dz` for every `t > 0`, where
    `K(t, y) = (2π t)^{-1/2} exp(−y² / (2 t))` is the standard heat kernel.

    Real derivation in `MathFin/Foundations/FeynmanKacHeatEquation.lean`:
    * `heatKernel`, `heatKernel_eq_gaussianPDFReal` (heat-kernel definition,
      identification with Mathlib's `gaussianPDFReal 0 t`),
    * `hasDerivAt_heatKernel_y`, `hasDerivAt_heatKernel_y_y`,
      `hasDerivAt_heatKernel_t` (first and second `y`-derivatives, first
      `t`-derivative via product / chain rule on `(2π t)^{-1/2}` and
      `exp(−y² / (2 t))`),
    * `heatKernel_t_eq_half_y_y` (the heat-kernel PDE
      `∂_t K = (1/2) ∂²_y K` as an algebraic identity on derivatives),
    * `feynmanU_eq_expectation` (heat-kernel convolution equals the BM
      expectation, via `Measure.map (B t) μ = gaussianReal 0 t.toNNReal`
      + `integral_gaussianReal_eq_integral_smul`
      + Lebesgue translation invariance via `integral_sub_right_eq_self`).

    Axioms: only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (Mathlib defaults).

    Scope note: this formalization derives the **Feynman–Kac formula**
    (heat-kernel convolution equals `E[g(x + B_t)]`) and the **boundary
    condition** (`u(0, x) = g(x)`). The full Saporito theorem also asserts
    that `u` satisfies the heat PDE `∂_t u − (1/2) ∂_xx u = 0`. The PDE
    direction reduces to applying `heatKernel_t_eq_half_y_y` under the integral
    via differentiation under the integral sign
    (`hasDerivAt_integral_of_dominated_loc_of_deriv_le`). That infrastructure is
    now proved in the imported file — `hasDerivAt_feynmanU_t/_x/_xx`,
    `hasDerivAt_integral_mul_kernelFamily`, and `feynmanU_heat_equation` — and
    is consumed by benchmark `sc-bs-pde-feynman-kac` (the Black–Scholes PDE via
    Feynman–Kac); the only remaining gap is the fully-general continuous-g PDE
    and uniqueness. -/
theorem feynman_kac_formula
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω}
    {B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {g : ℝ → ℝ}
    (hB_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (B t))
    (hB_zero : ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, B 0 ω = 0)
    (hB_gauss : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t →
      ∃ v : NNReal, (v : ℝ) = t - s ∧
        Measure.map (fun ω => B t ω - B s ω) μ = gaussianReal 0 v)
    (hg_cont : Continuous g)
    {t : ℝ} (ht : 0 < t) (x : ℝ) :
    MathFin.FeynmanKacHeatEquation.feynmanU g t x = ∫ ω, g (x + B t ω) ∂μ :=
  MathFin.FeynmanKacHeatEquation.feynmanU_eq_expectation
    hB_meas hB_zero hB_gauss hg_cont ht x

/-- Theorem 9.2.1 (Boundary condition). At `t = 0`, the Feynman–Kac expectation
    `E[g(x + B_0)] = g(x)`. -/
theorem feynman_kac_boundary
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {g : ℝ → ℝ}
    (hB_zero : ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, B 0 ω = 0) (x : ℝ) :
    ∫ ω, g (x + B 0 ω) ∂μ = g x :=
  MathFin.FeynmanKacHeatEquation.feynmanKac_boundary hB_zero x

metadata.reference: Theorem 9.2.1
metadata.formalization_scope: Full derivation of the Feynman–Kac formula direction. The heat-kernel convolution `∫ z, g(z) · K(t, z − x) ∂volume` is proved equal to the BM expectation `E[g(x + B_t)]` from primitives (`B_0 = 0` a.s., Gaussian increments, `g` continuous, `t > 0`) via Measure.map transfer through `gaussianReal 0 t.toNNReal` + density representation + Lebesgue translation invariance. Boundary condition `u(0, x) = g(x)` derived from `B_0 = 0` + probability measure. Heat-kernel PDE on `K` (`∂_t K = (1/2) ∂²_y K`) is also proved as part of the supporting infrastructure (via product/chain rule on `(2π t)^{-1/2} exp(-y²/(2t))`). Scope note: the heat-equation PDE for `u` is not bundled into this snippet, but the infrastructure it needs — the kernel-side derivatives, the parametric differentiate-under-the-integral skeleton, and the heat equation `∂_t u = ½ ∂_xx u` for sub-exponential payoffs — is now proved in the imported file (`hasDerivAt_feynmanU_{t,x,xx}`, `hasDerivAt_integral_mul_kernelFamily`, `feynmanU_heat_equation`) and consumed by the benchmark `sc-bs-pde-feynman-kac` (the Black–Scholes PDE via Feynman–Kac). The remaining gap is the fully-general continuous-`g` PDE + uniqueness. Axioms: only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

theorems.name: Existence and Uniqueness of SDE Solutions
theorems.description: Theorem 8.2.5: Under linear growth and Lipschitz conditions on (μ, σ), the SDE dX = μ(X) dt + σ(X) dB with X_0 = η has a unique strong solution

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- SDE existence-and-uniqueness specification: drift coefficient `μ_coef` and
    diffusion coefficient `σ_coef` that are Lipschitz with linear growth, an
    initial condition `η`, the Brownian driver `B`, and the conclusion that the
    SDE `dX = μ_coef(X) dt + σ_coef(X) dB`, `X_0 = η` has a unique strong
    solution (Theorem 8.2.5).

    The diffusion term is the Itô stochastic integral, carried as an *opaque
    integral operator* `Iσ` on candidate processes (read
    `Iσ Z = ∫₀^· σ_coef(Z_s) dB_s`) rather than written as a Lebesgue integral
    `∫ σ_coef ds` — the latter would encode a different, non-stochastic
    equation. The Itô integral against `B` is not available in Mathlib at the
    pinned toolchain, so the operator is opaque: its construction against `B`
    (adaptedness, isometry) is not modeled, and `B` appears only as the named
    driver the operator notionally integrates against. What the structure does
    pin is how existence and uniqueness consume the operator: the solution
    field uses `Iσ X`, and the uniqueness field constrains candidates `Y`
    through the *same* operator `Iσ Y` — candidates must solve the same
    equation, not an arbitrary one. (Quantifying a free per-candidate integral
    instead would let every process discharge the solution premise by choosing
    its own residual as "integral", collapsing the field to a falsehood; the
    operator form keeps the spec inhabited — see the example below.) The drift
    term remains a genuine Lebesgue integral `∫₀ᵗ μ_coef(Y_s) ds`. -/
structure SDEExistenceUniqueness {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (P : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure P] (B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ)
    (μ_coef σ_coef : ℝ → ℝ) (η : Ω → ℝ) (X : ℝ → Ω → ℝ)
    (Iσ : (ℝ → Ω → ℝ) → ℝ → Ω → ℝ) : Prop where
  /-- μ_coef is Lipschitz. -/
  mu_lipschitz : ∃ K : ℝ, ∀ x y, |μ_coef x - μ_coef y| ≤ K * |x - y|
  /-- σ_coef is Lipschitz. -/
  sigma_lipschitz : ∃ K : ℝ, ∀ x y, |σ_coef x - σ_coef y| ≤ K * |x - y|
  /-- Linear growth conditions. -/
  mu_linear_growth : ∃ C : ℝ, ∀ x, |μ_coef x| ≤ C * (1 + |x|)
  sigma_linear_growth : ∃ C : ℝ, ∀ x, |σ_coef x| ≤ C * (1 + |x|)
  /-- Initial condition matches. -/
  initial_condition : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, X 0 ω = η ω
  /-- Theorem 8.2.5 (existence): `X` solves the SDE, the diffusion entering
      through the opaque Itô-integral operator evaluated at `X`. -/
  sde_solution : ∀ ⦃t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ t →
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂P,
      X t ω = η ω + (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, μ_coef (X s ω)) + Iσ X t ω
  /-- Theorem 8.2.5 (uniqueness): any candidate `Y` solving the same SDE —
      same drift, same diffusion operator — agrees with `X` almost surely at
      every nonnegative time. -/
  uniqueness : ∀ (Y : ℝ → Ω → ℝ),
    (∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Y 0 ω = η ω) →
    (∀ ⦃t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ t →
      ∀ᵐ ω ∂P,
        Y t ω = η ω + (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, μ_coef (Y s ω)) + Iσ Y t ω) →
    ∀ ⦃t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ t → ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Y t ω = X t ω

/-- Theorem 8.2.5 (existence and uniqueness for SDEs): under Lipschitz and
    linear-growth assumptions on the drift and diffusion coefficients, the SDE
    `dX = μ_coef(X) dt + σ_coef(X) dB` with initial condition `X_0 = η` has a
    unique strong solution. The diffusion enters through the opaque
    Itô-integral operator `Iσ`. -/
theorem SDEExistenceUniqueness.unique_strong_solution
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {μ_coef σ_coef : ℝ → ℝ} {η : Ω → ℝ} {X : ℝ → Ω → ℝ}
    {Iσ : (ℝ → Ω → ℝ) → ℝ → Ω → ℝ}
    (h : SDEExistenceUniqueness P B μ_coef σ_coef η X Iσ) (Y : ℝ → Ω → ℝ)
    (hY0 : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Y 0 ω = η ω)
    (hY :
      ∀ ⦃t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ t →
        ∀ᵐ ω ∂P,
          Y t ω = η ω + (∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, μ_coef (Y s ω)) + Iσ Y t ω) :
    ∀ ⦃t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ t → ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Y t ω = X t ω :=
  h.uniqueness Y hY0 hY

/-- Non-vacuity guard: the specification is inhabited — the deterministic
    zero SDE on the one-point space, with the zero integral operator. -/
example : SDEExistenceUniqueness (Measure.dirac ()) (B := fun _ _ => 0)
    (μ_coef := fun _ => 0) (σ_coef := fun _ => 0) (η := fun _ => 0)
    (X := fun _ _ => 0) (Iσ := fun _ _ _ => 0) where
  mu_lipschitz := ⟨0, by simp⟩
  sigma_lipschitz := ⟨0, by simp⟩
  mu_linear_growth := ⟨0, by simp⟩
  sigma_linear_growth := ⟨0, by simp⟩
  initial_condition := by simp
  sde_solution := by intro t ht; simp
  uniqueness := by
    intro Y hY0 hY t ht
    filter_upwards [hY ht] with ω hω
    simpa using hω

The diffusion is carried by an opaque integral operator Iσ (read: Z ↦ ∫₀^· σ_coef(Z_s) dB_s): the solution field consumes Iσ X and the uniqueness field pins candidates through the same Iσ Y, so candidates genuinely solve the same equation; the operator's Itô construction against B (adaptedness, isometry) is not modeled, and B is a named driver only. theorems.name: Black-Scholes PDE
theorems.description: Chapter 10: The Black-Scholes PDE: ∂V/∂t + (1/2)σ²S²∂²V/∂S² + rS∂V/∂S - rV = 0

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.PDE

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real

/-- Theorem (Black–Scholes PDE, forward direction).
    The European call price
    `V(S, t) = S · Φ(d₁(S, K, r, σ, T−t)) − K · e^{−r(T−t)} · Φ(d₂(S, K, r, σ, T−t))`
    satisfies the Black–Scholes PDE `∂V/∂t + (1/2) σ² S² ∂²V/∂S² + r S ∂V/∂S − r V = 0`
    for any `S > 0, t < T, K > 0, σ > 0`.

    Real derivation in `MathFin/BlackScholes/PDE.lean`:
    * `hasDerivAt_Phi` (in `GaussianCDFDeriv.lean`): standard normal CDF
      derivative `Φ' = ϕ` via FTC on `Iic`-Lebesgue decomposition +
      `intervalIntegral.integral_hasDerivAt_right`.
    * `bs_identity`: the Black–Scholes magic identity
      `S · ϕ(d₁) = K · e^{−rτ} · ϕ(d₂)`, derived from the explicit
      formulae for `d₁, d₂` and `exp(log(S/K)) = S/K`.
    * `hasDerivAt_bsd1_S, hasDerivAt_bsd2_S`: `∂_S d_i = 1/(S σ √τ)`,
      both i = 1, 2.
    * `hasDerivAt_bsd1_tau, hasDerivAt_bsd2_tau`: τ-derivatives of d₁, d₂
      via quotient rule.
    * `hasDerivAt_bsV_S`: `∂_S V = Φ(d₁)` (the Delta — magic identity
      collapses the `S · ϕ(d₁) − K e^{−rτ} · ϕ(d₂)` term).
    * `hasDerivAt_bsV_SS`: `∂²_S V = ϕ(d₁)/(S σ √τ)` (the Gamma).
    * `hasDerivAt_bsV_tau`: `∂_τ V = σ S ϕ(d₁)/(2√τ) + r K e^{−rτ} Φ(d₂)` —
      magic identity collapses `pdf(d₂) (∂_τ d₂)` into `S φ(d₁) ∂_τ d₂`,
      leaving `S φ(d₁) (∂_τ d₁ − ∂_τ d₂) = S φ(d₁) · σ/(2√τ)`.
    * `hasDerivAt_bsV_t`: `∂_t V = −∂_τ V` (chain rule on `τ = T − t`).
    * `bs_pde_holds`: the PDE identity itself — purely algebraic after
      substituting the closed forms (no Itô calculus required).

    Axioms: only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (Mathlib defaults).
    Confirmed via `#print axioms` on every supporting lemma. -/
theorem bs_pde_satisfied (K T r σ : ℝ) (hK : 0 < K) (hσ : 0 < σ)
    {S t : ℝ} (hS : 0 < S) (ht_T : t < T) :
    HasDerivAt (fun t' => MathFin.bsV K r σ S (T - t'))
      (-(σ * S * gaussianPDFReal 0 1 (MathFin.bsd1 S K r σ (T - t))
          / (2 * Real.sqrt (T - t))
        + r * K * Real.exp (-(r * (T - t))) *
            MathFin.Phi (MathFin.bsd2 S K r σ (T - t)))) t ∧
    HasDerivAt (fun s => MathFin.bsV K r σ s (T - t))
      (MathFin.Phi (MathFin.bsd1 S K r σ (T - t))) S ∧
    HasDerivAt (fun s => MathFin.Phi (MathFin.bsd1 s K r σ (T - t)))
      (gaussianPDFReal 0 1 (MathFin.bsd1 S K r σ (T - t)) /
        (S * σ * Real.sqrt (T - t))) S ∧
    -(σ * S * gaussianPDFReal 0 1 (MathFin.bsd1 S K r σ (T - t))
        / (2 * Real.sqrt (T - t))
      + r * K * Real.exp (-(r * (T - t))) *
          MathFin.Phi (MathFin.bsd2 S K r σ (T - t))) +
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) * σ ^ 2 * S ^ 2 *
      (gaussianPDFReal 0 1 (MathFin.bsd1 S K r σ (T - t)) /
        (S * σ * Real.sqrt (T - t))) +
    r * S * MathFin.Phi (MathFin.bsd1 S K r σ (T - t)) -
    r * MathFin.bsV K r σ S (T - t) = 0 :=
  have hτ : 0 < T - t := sub_pos.mpr ht_T
  ⟨MathFin.hasDerivAt_bsV_t hK hσ hS ht_T,
   MathFin.hasDerivAt_bsV_S hK hσ hS hτ,
   MathFin.hasDerivAt_bsV_SS hK hσ hS hτ,
   MathFin.bs_pde_holds hσ hS ht_T⟩

theorems.name: Black-Scholes PDE via Feynman-Kac
theorems.description: Chapter 9/10: The Black-Scholes PDE derived from the Feynman–Kac representation — the operator vanishes because the heat kernel does (independent of the closed-form route in sc-bs-pde).

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.PDEFromFeynmanKac

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real

/-- Theorem (Black–Scholes PDE via Feynman–Kac).
    The European call value `bsV K r σ S τ` satisfies the Black–Scholes operator
    `−∂_τ V + (1/2) σ² S² ∂²_S V + r S ∂_S V − r V = 0`, with all three derivatives
    supplied by the Feynman–Kac Greeks — an independent re-derivation of `bs_pde_holds`
    grounded in the heat kernel rather than the closed-form Φ.

    Real derivation in `MathFin/BlackScholes/PDEFromFeynmanKac.lean` and
    `MathFin/Foundations/FeynmanKacHeatEquation.lean`:
    * `hasFDerivAt_heatKernel`: the heat kernel is jointly Fréchet-differentiable in (t, y).
    * `hasDerivAt_feynmanU_comp`: the Feynman–Kac function differentiated along the curve
      `τ ↦ (σ²τ, log S + (r−σ²/2)τ)`, both kernel arguments moving.
    * `hasDerivAt_bsV_S_fk`, `hasDerivAt_bsV_SS_fk`, `hasDerivAt_bsV_tau_fk`: the FK Greeks
      Δ, Γ, Θ as heat-kernel moments of the call payoff.
    * `feynmanU_heat_equation`: the kernel heat equation `∂_t U = ½ ∂_xx U`.
    * `bsV_satisfies_bs_pde_via_feynmanKac`: the operator vanishes because the heat kernel
      does — the `U_x` drift terms cancel algebraically and `∂_t U = ½ ∂_xx U`.

    Axioms: only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (Mathlib defaults). -/
theorem bs_pde_via_feynman_kac {K r σ : ℝ} (hK : 0 < K) (hσ : 0 < σ)
    {S τ : ℝ} (hS : 0 < S) (hτ : 0 < τ) :
    ∃ Vτ VS VSS : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun τ' => MathFin.bsV K r σ S τ') Vτ τ ∧
      HasDerivAt (fun S' => MathFin.bsV K r σ S' τ) VS S ∧
      HasDerivAt (fun S' => deriv (fun S'' => MathFin.bsV K r σ S'' τ) S') VSS S ∧
      -Vτ + (1 / 2) * σ ^ 2 * S ^ 2 * VSS + r * S * VS - r * MathFin.bsV K r σ S τ = 0 :=
  MathFin.bsV_satisfies_bs_pde_via_feynmanKac hK hσ hS hτ

metadata.reference: Black-Scholes PDE via Feynman-Kac
metadata.formalization_scope: Independent, probabilistically-grounded re-derivation of the BS PDE (cf. sc-bs-pde, the closed-form route). The call value V = e^{-rτ}·feynmanU(call payoff) is a discounted Gaussian heat flow; the three derivatives are the Feynman–Kac Greeks (hasDerivAt_bsV_{S,SS,tau}_fk), each a heat-kernel integral built on the heat kernel's joint Fréchet differentiability (hasFDerivAt_heatKernel) → curve derivative (hasDerivAt_feynmanU_comp, via the parametric diff-under-integral skeleton + a sum-of-two-Gaussian-moment-envelopes domination). The operator vanishes because ∂_t U = ½ ∂_xx U (feynmanU_heat_equation, the kernel heat equation) and the U_x drift terms cancel exactly. Closes the two-tower gap: feynmanU is now load-bearing for pricing. Axioms: only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

theorems.name: Discrete Ito Identity for Cubing
theorems.description: Pathwise X_N^3 - X_0^3 decomposition into the third-power discrete Ito integral (3 sum X^2 dX), the QV-weighted cross sum (3 sum X (dX)^2), and the cubic remainder. Specialisation of the discrete Ito formula to f(x)=x^3.

import MathFin.Foundations.DiscreteItoPolynomial

open MathFin

theorem discrete_cubing_identity_thm (N : ℕ) (X : ℕ → ℝ) :
    X N ^ 3 - X 0 ^ 3 =
      3 * (∑ k ∈ Finset.range N, X k ^ 2 * (X (k + 1) - X k)) +
      3 * (∑ k ∈ Finset.range N, X k * (X (k + 1) - X k) ^ 2) +
      ∑ k ∈ Finset.range N, (X (k + 1) - X k) ^ 3 :=
  MathFin.discrete_cubing_identity N X

theorems.name: Time-Indexed Ito Isometry on Simple Processes
theorems.description: The time-indexed Ito isometry: for a simple adapted process V, E[(V dot B)_t^2] equals the predictable-rectangle double sum with every interval endpoint truncated at t. The process form of the terminal Ito isometry that the continuous L2 Ito-integral construction rests on.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ItoIntegralProcessMartingale

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory MathFin MathFin.ItoIntegralProcess
open scoped NNReal ENNReal

theorem itoSimpleProcess_isometry_time_thm
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {μ : Measure Ω}
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ)
    (hBmeas : ∀ t, Measurable (B t))
    (V : SimpleProcess ℝ (ItoIntegralL2.natFiltration (mΩ := mΩ) hBmeas)) (t : ℝ≥0) :
    ∫ ω, (itoSimpleProcess hBmeas V t ω) ^ 2 ∂μ
      = ∑ p ∈ V.value.support, ∑ q ∈ V.value.support,
          (∫ ω, V.value p ω * V.value q ω ∂μ)
            * max 0 ((min ((min p.2 t : ℝ≥0) : ℝ) ((min q.2 t : ℝ≥0) : ℝ))
                - (max ((min p.1 t : ℝ≥0) : ℝ) ((min q.1 t : ℝ≥0) : ℝ))) :=
  itoSimpleProcess_isometry_time hB hBmeas V t

metadata.reference: Time-indexed Ito isometry on simple processes (Saporito Ch 7)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Re-export from MathFin.Foundations.ItoIntegralProcessMartingale (itoSimpleProcess_isometry_time). E[(V dot B)_t^2] = sum over predictable rectangles (p,q) of E[V(p)*V(q)] * vol of the overlap of the t-truncated intervals; mathematically, the terminal itoSimple_sq_integral is recovered when t is past all right endpoints. Cross terms vanish by the weak Markov property, diagonal terms give the deterministic overlap (rect_increment_pairing), rectangles whose left endpoint is past t collapse to zero. Axioms-clean.

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Re-export from MathFin.Foundations.ItoIntegralProcessLocalMartingale (itoSimpleProcess_isLocalMartingale). Two ingredients: (1) pathwise continuity itoSimpleProcess_pathContinuous -- for each omega, t -> (V dot B)_t omega = sum_p V(p)omega*(B_{p.2 cap t}omega - B_{p.1 cap t}omega) (itoSimpleProcess_apply) is a finite sum of the continuous Brownian path B(.)omega composed with the continuous clamp t -> min p.i t, hence continuous; (2) the martingale property (itoSimpleProcess_isMartingale, B1a). Continuity gives cadlag paths, so Degenne's Martingale.IsLocalMartingale (Locally.of_prop along the constant localizing sequence, sorry-free) places the integral in the local-martingale class. The continuity of B is taken as a hypothesis -- the standard pathwise setting; IsPreBrownian fixes only the finite-dimensional laws and a continuous version exists by Kolmogorov-Chentsov. Honest scope: elementary (simple) integrands, for which the integral is in fact a true martingale, so the local-martingale statement is the canonical framing connecting to the localized theory; the genuinely new content is the pathwise continuity. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Covariation of Ito Integrals: the Bilinear Ito Isometry
theorems.description: The expectation of the product of two Ito integrals equals the L2(trim) inner product of their integrands: E_mu[(int phi dB)(int psi dB)] = <phi, psi> = int phi*psi d(trim) = E_mu int_0^T phi_s psi_s ds. This is the bilinear (polarized) companion of the Ito isometry: the Ito integral CLM, bundled as a LinearIsometry, preserves the L2 inner product (LinearIsometry.inner_map_map), not just the norm. The diagonal phi = psi recovers the Ito isometry E[(int phi dB)^2] = ||phi||^2. The covariance backbone for multi-factor / covariance-swap pricing.

The finite-horizon Ito integral itoIntegralCLM_T is bundled as a LinearIsometry itoIsometry_T from the existing norm isometry itoIntegralCLM_T_norm (the norm_map' field, rewriting the ContinuousLinearMap->LinearMap coercion rather than forcing a defeq through the extendOfNorm term). LinearIsometry.inner_map_map (a real linear norm-isometry preserves the inner product by the polarization identity -- pure Mathlib) then gives <int phi dB, int psi dB> = <phi, psi>; L2.inner_def and the real inner product = multiplication (RCLike.inner_apply, conj_trivial) unfold the mu-side to the expectation E[(int phi dB)(int psi dB)]. The diagonal corollary variance_itoIntegralCLM_T recovers the Ito isometry via real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq. Axioms-clean.

This is the first pathwise (sample-path) regularity result for the GENERAL integrand (B3 gave it only for simple integrands) and the localization gateway for the unbounded-coefficient Ito calculus. Non-redundant with Degenne's general cadlag modification, which is sorry-backed; the L2 + Doob route yields a genuinely continuous (not merely cadlag) version.

theorem exists_continuous_modification_itoProcess_thm
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ)

metadata.reference: Continuous modification of the stochastic integral / pathwise regularity (Saporito Ch 7)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Re-export from MathFin.Foundations.ItoIntegralProcessContinuousModification (exists_continuous_modification_itoProcess). Construction: simple approximants V_n (approxSeq, from density simpleAssembly_T_denseRange, ||simpleAssembly_T V_n - phi|| <= 2^-n) each have continuous paths (B3 itoSimpleProcess_pathContinuous). Degenne's continuous-time weak-type maximal inequality maximal_ineq_norm applied to the continuous L2 martingale (V_n - V_{n+1}) dot B (itoSimpleProcess_maximal_weak), then the Chebyshev/L2 terminal-norm form (itoSimpleProcess_maximal_prob, via L1<=L2 on the probability space and the terminal Ito isometry), give a summable maximal tail (summable_maximal_tail, eps_n = (3/4)^n). Borel-Cantelli (ae_eventually_notMem) gives ae eventual smallness; the running-max keystone norm_le_iSup_Iic (compactness of [0,T], norm bounded by the subtype supremum) plus linearity make the subsequence a.s.-uniformly Cauchy, so the uniform limit itoContinuousMod = limUnder is pathwise continuous (itoContinuousMod_continuousOn, TendstoUniformlyOn.continuousOn with a shift-indexed geometric tail bound 4*(3/4)^n uniform in t). The modification identity itoContinuousMod_modification (forall t <= T, X t =ae itoProcessCLM T t phi) is tendstoInMeasure_ae_unique applied to the ae limit (tendstoInMeasure_of_tendsto_ae) and the L2 limit (tendstoInMeasure_of_tendsto_eLpNorm from the CLM-continuity Lp convergence via the bridge itoProcessCLM_simpleAssembly_T). Honest scope: finite horizon [0,T]; the [0,infty) version, the localized Ito formula for unbounded coefficients, and the IsLocalMartingale framing (which needs a null-augmented (complete) filtration for the everywhere-cadlag adaptedness, infrastructure natFiltration lacks) are explicit follow-ons. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: The General-Integrand Ito Process is a Continuous Local Martingale on [0,T]
theorems.description: The continuous modification of the general-integrand Ito process (the gate) is upgraded to a genuine local martingale. There is a process X : R>=0 -> Omega -> R that (i) is a MODIFICATION of the L2 Ito process (X t =ae itoProcessCLM T t phi for every t <= T), (ii) has EVERYWHERE-continuous sample paths, and (iii) is an IsLocalMartingale for the NULL-AUGMENTED Brownian filtration natFiltration ⊔ nulls. Degenne's Martingale.IsLocalMartingale requires cadlag paths for every omega, which forces an everywhere-continuous adapted representative; the a.e.-continuous modification can be repaired into one only because the augmented filtration carries the mu-null sets. The measure-theoretic heart is condExp_sup_nulls: conditioning on the null augmentation agrees a.e. with conditioning on the Brownian filtration, which transfers the L2 martingale property to the repaired process. Non-redundant: Degenne's general cadlag modification is sorry-backed; this yields an everywhere-continuous local martingale, the localization interface the unbounded-coefficient Ito calculus is built to consume.

metadata.reference: The stochastic integral as a continuous local martingale / usual conditions (Saporito Ch 7)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Re-export from MathFin.Foundations.ItoIntegralProcessLocalMartingaleGeneral (exists_continuous_localMartingale_modification). Upgrades the gate (exists_continuous_modification_itoProcess, an a.e.-continuous modification) to Degenne's IsLocalMartingale interface. Degenne's Martingale.IsLocalMartingale needs cadlag paths for EVERY omega, which forces an everywhere-continuous representative; on a null set this can only be repaired while staying adapted if the filtration carries the null sets. The file builds the NULL-AUGMENTED Brownian filtration augFiltration = natFiltration ⊔ Filtration.const (nullsAlg), and the measure-theoretic core condExp_sup_nulls (conditioning on the augmentation agrees a.e. with conditioning on natFiltration), proved via the sigma-algebra crux exists_ae_eq_of_sup_nulls (every (m1 ⊔ nulls)-set is a.e. an m1-set). The representative itoLocalMod G T t = G.indicator (itoContinuousMod (min t T)) clamps the modification to [0,T] via min and zeroes off a measurable co-null good set G (extracted via exists_measurable_superset_of_null from the a.e. convergence + continuity sets); it is everywhere continuous, a modification (X t =ae itoProcessCLM T t phi for t <= T), adapted to augFiltration (stronglyMeasurable_of_tendsto on the pointwise limit of G.indicator of the simple integrals, each natFiltration-measurable), and adapted (also exposed as a fourth conclusion StronglyMeasurable[augFiltration i] for downstream consumers such as the [0,infty) gluing), and an augmented martingale (condExp_congr_ae + condExp_sup_nulls + the L2 martingale identity itoIntegralProcessGen_isMartingale at clamped indices). Honest scope: finite horizon [0,T]; the [0,infty) crown is delivered separately (sc-ito-infinite-local-martingale, which consumes this result's per-horizon adaptedness). The IsLocalMartingale for the unaugmented natFiltration is not achievable (the repaired representative is not natFiltration-measurable) and stays out of scope. Axioms-clean: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

theorems.name: The Unbounded-Horizon Ito Integral is a Continuous Local Martingale on [0,infty)
theorems.description: The unbounded-horizon Ito integral is a continuous local martingale on the WHOLE half-line R>=0. There is a process X that (i) is a MODIFICATION of the unbounded-horizon L2 process at EVERY t (X t =ae itoProcessL2Inf t f), (ii) has EVERYWHERE-continuous sample paths on all of R>=0, and (iii) is an IsLocalMartingale for the null-augmented Brownian filtration. Per horizon T=n the finite follow-on (exists_continuous_localMartingale_modification) gives an everywhere-continuous local-martingale modification Xn of the [0,n]-band-restricted integrand; horizon consistency (itoProcessL2Inf_eq_itoProcessCLM) makes every Xn a modification of the SAME unbounded-horizon process on [0,n], so on a co-null set the Xn agree on overlaps (indistinguishable_of_modification_on). Gluing them -- at time t read horizon ceil(t)+1, strictly above t -- yields a single process continuous on all of R>=0, a modification at every t, and adapted to the augmentation. Unlike the [0,T] follow-on there is NO horizon clamp: the martingale property holds globally on R>=0, supplied directly by itoProcessL2Inf_isMartingale through condExp_sup_nulls, because the unbounded-horizon process is genuinely a martingale on the whole half-line. This is the [0,infty) crown of the continuous-time Ito tower: the stochastic integral as a continuous local martingale on the entire time domain.

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Re-export from MathFin.Foundations.ItoIntegralProcessLocalMartingaleInfinite (exists_continuous_localMartingale_modification_infinite). Builds the [0,infty) continuous local martingale by gluing the per-horizon [0,T=n] continuous local martingales (the strengthened finite follow-on, which now also exposes per-horizon StronglyMeasurable[augFiltration] adaptedness). The crux is horizon consistency itoProcessL2Inf_eq_itoProcessCLM (the [0,infty) process restricted to [0,T] = the finite-horizon process of the band-restricted integrand), proved via a hand-built [0,T] clamp of Degenne's SimpleProcess (clampSP: drop intervals starting past T, clamp surviving right endpoints) with a shared clampSP_value_sum identity driving both the discrete-integral and L2-integrand agreements, then a DenseRange.equalizer. The glue: indistinguishable_of_modification_on on each overlap [0,m) gives pairwise pathwise agreement on a co-null set (countable intersection over horizon pairs via ae_all_iff + exists_measurable_superset_of_null); reading horizon ceil(t)+1 at time t defines a single path continuous on every [0,K) hence on all of R>=0. Adaptedness: the per-horizon adaptedness through the indicator of the augmentation-measurable good set. Martingale: the GLOBAL itoProcessL2Inf_isMartingale transported across the augmentation by condExp_sup_nulls (no clamp needed). Axioms-clean: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

theorems.name: Wiener Integral of a Deterministic Integrand is Gaussian
theorems.description: The Wiener integral of a DETERMINISTIC L2 integrand is Gaussian: for f in L2((0,T]), omega |-> (wienerIntegralLp B hB T f) omega has law gaussianReal 0 ||f||^2 -- centred, variance the squared L2 norm (the Ito isometry value). This is the distributional content the isometry construction left open: WienerIntegralL2 pinned the NORM of the integral but never its LAW. Proof by characteristic functions: on simple (step) processes the integral is a finite linear combination of the Gaussian process B, hence Gaussian (IsGaussianProcess.of_isGaussianProcess + map_eq_gaussianReal: mean 0 from centred increments, variance the isometry via wiener_assembly_isometry); the map g |-> charFun (mu.map (wienerIntegralLp g)) t is |t|-Lipschitz in the L2 random variable, so it agrees with exp(-||g||^2 t^2 / 2) on the dense simple processes (DenseRange.induction_on), and Measure.ext_of_charFun lifts the pointwise characteristic-function identity to the law. The structural fact behind every linear-SDE terminal law (Vasicek / Ornstein-Uhlenbeck / Hull-White); first consumed by the Vasicek terminal law in FixedIncome.
